mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5% signals strong labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 5.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and declining housing starts signal a supply-side shock that increases corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST shows a negative coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions, or 60-day returns in combined growth scenarios fall below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle, indicated by rising federal funds rates above 4.5%, compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS in 3-year regressions is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls above 150k signals robust employment growth that supports consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or the 5-day return volatility exceeds 2.5% in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million units signals strong demand for construction, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1200, or the correlation between HOUST and SPX returns falls below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) and low policy rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) create a stagflationary environment that depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows a positive coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions, or 60-day returns in stagflation scenarios exceed 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A supply-side shock, indicated by a 3-month decline in industrial production below 100, reduces corporate output and raises input costs, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 100, or the coefficient on INDPRO in 3-year regressions is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
